metadata.description: Extensions that bring Claude-Code-style capabilities to JetBrains Junie CLI: curated subagent orchestration, cross-session memory, and lazy-senior-dev mode.

extensions.name: junie-subagents
extensions.description: A curated set of focused subagents (explorer, planner, implementer, reviewer, test-runner) modeled on Claude Code's built-in agents, plus a guideline that teaches Junie's main agent to decompose complex tasks and delegate them — the closest practical equivalent to Claude Code's create/process/orchestrate subagent workflow.

extensions.name: junie-memory
extensions.description: A cross-session persona memory for Junie CLI, modeled on Claude Code's memory: an auto-loaded guideline that teaches the agent to recall at the start of a task and persist user preferences and working-style notes (user / feedback / reference) as files under ~/.junie/memory/, plus /remember and /memories slash commands. Always-injected small index — file convention, no server.

extensions.name: junie-knowledge
extensions.description: A cross-session knowledge store for Junie CLI that cuts agent token usage: an auto-loaded guideline that tells the agent to search before re-reading large files and to save distilled project facts, codebase maps, and session recaps (the single home for durable project facts — persona/preferences stay in junie-memory) via a shared MCP server with semantic retrieval, plus a /knowledge browse command. Distil at save time, recall on demand — backed by SQLite + local embeddings.

extensions.name: junie-ponytail
extensions.description: Lazy senior dev mode for Junie CLI — forces the laziest solution that works: YAGNI, reuse, stdlib and native platform features first, one line over fifty. Adapted from DietrichGebert/ponytail (MIT): an always-on guideline, six auto-invoked skills (ponytail, review, audit, debt, gain, help), and matching slash commands.

extensions.name: junie-plane
extensions.description: Drive a self-hosted Plane issue/ticket tracker from Junie CLI via a thin scripts/plane.sh helper (curl + jq) — fetch, create, move, comment on, and triage issues and cycles. No MCP. An auto-invoked skill documents the workspace/project/state model.

extensions.name: junie-codeberg
extensions.description: Drive Codeberg source hosting from Junie CLI via the `fj` (forgejo-cli) plus git — auth, feature branches, in-repo worktrees under .worktrees/, pull requests, squash-merges, issues, labels, and Woodpecker CI status.
